programme.title: The National
programme.desc: CBC's flagship nightly news program, featuring the day's top stories with in-depth and original journalism; Adrienne Arsenault and Ian Hanomansing anchor.

programme.title: CBC News: Morning
programme.desc: What do you need to know about the world around you? Heather Hiscox takes the helm as the anchor for this weekday-morning news program to tell you. Joining her is Colleen Jones with weather and sports info., and Danielle Bochove with all you need to know about the business world.

programme.desc: Andrew Chang expands the understanding of the stories everybody's talking about.

programme.title: CTV News Special: Rick Hansen: Unbreakable 50 Years Later
programme.desc: Rick Hansen returns to the scene of the accident that changed his life forever. CTV’s Sandie Rinaldo talks to the Canadian icon.

programme.title: W5: Avery Haines Investigates
programme.desc: Avery rides atop Mexico’s “death train” through narco territory, documenting the harrowing journey migrants take in search of a better life.

programme.title: CTV News Special: Rick Hansen: Unbreakable 50 Years Later
programme.desc: Rick Hansen returns to the scene of the accident that changed his life forever. CTV’s Sandie Rinaldo talks to the Canadian icon.

programme.title: Question Period
programme.desc: Question Period, the national political landmark program, is a must-see for political junkies.

programme.desc: Rick Hansen returns to the scene of the accident that changed his life forever. CTV’s Sandie Rinaldo talks to the Canadian icon.

programme.desc: Avery rides atop Mexico’s “death train” through narco territory, documenting the harrowing journey migrants take in search of a better life.

programme.desc: An investigator uses new scientific advances to track a 16-year-old girl's murderer, 50 years later.

programme.title: W5

programme.title: Country House Hunters
programme.desc: On today's episode we meet young couple Ash & Ollie, they are on the search for their dream home in Kiama.

programme.title: Country House Hunters
programme.desc: Newly married couple Azriel & Eloraare are looking for a property with suitable acreage for horses.

programme.title: Country House Hunters: Canada
programme.desc: Searching for the perfect mountain property for Andy and Wendy in the country town of Canmore.

programme.title: Country House Hunters: Canada
programme.desc: Today we are in the stunning Blue Mountains searching for a for ever home for Susan and Al. Will they find what they are looking for?

programme.title: Country House Hunters: Canada
programme.desc: Set between the beautiful mountain scape, Blue River has it all. Will Sarah and Tate find their dream home?

programme.title: Country House Hunters: Canada
programme.desc: Today we are on the hunt for a first home for newly weds Ben & Amber. Will they find the home to expand their family with?

programme.title: Country House Hunters: Canada
programme.desc: Carla and Marilo want to find the perfect hideaway in Nova Scotia.

programme.title: Country House Hunters: Canada
programme.desc: Heather and her mom, Diane are looking for the perfect family home.

programme.title: Country House Hunters: Canada
programme.desc: Janine and Trevor go to Snow Valley to search for a home, and the mountains may have what they need.

programme.title: Cross Country Cake Off
programme.desc: Mary and Andrew rendezvous in Montreal to taste eight Quebecers’ cakes. The top four bake “After Dark” cakes for a spot in the finale.

programme.title: Cross Country Cake Off
programme.desc: The qualifying round ends in the East with eight Atlantic cake makers. Four are chosen to bake their “Bucket List” cake for the judges.

programme.title: Cross Country Cake Off
programme.desc: The top cake makers from each region come together in the CROSS COUNTRY CAKE OFF kitchen to bake for the grand prize in the national finale.

programme.title: Cross Country Cake Off
programme.desc: Bakers from the West present their best cakes to judges Mary Berg and Andrew Han. The top four bake Outdoor Adventure cakes to continue on.

programme.title: Cross Country Cake Off
programme.desc: The Prairies’ top cake makers meet the judges in Edmonton and four are chosen to bring a “Water World” to life for a spot in the finale.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Hana teaches us the secrets behind forming the delicate tofu pockets, and why the pockets are named after the Shinto god Inari.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Thai cookbook author and chef Pailin Chongchitnant will show you how to make curry at home from scratch.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Surprisingly, Thais don't grill much at home, although they love to buy grilled food from street vendors.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Thai Food Inspired by Other Cultures
programme.desc: Thai chef Pailin Chongchitnant shows us some Thai dishes that were inspired by food from other cultures.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: It’s all about street food in this Vietnamese episode.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Mary Tang cooks up Cantonese recipes perfect for days when you need comfort food.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Fun host Lisa Nguyen shows us just how versatile noodles are.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Lisa Nguyen shows us recipes from Vietnam's capital city of Hanoi.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Home cook Mary Tang shows us popular Hong Kong culinary delights.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Olsen Europe makeover; actors Sean Cullen, Debra DiGiovanni and Darrin Rose.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Real Estate Reality Check with The Property Brothers!

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Mommy Bloggers & how to get deals online; fashion show with online buys; latest style apps.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Sally Field; The Canadian Tenors; Halloween meltdown survival Guide, Halloween nutrition myths.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Lloyd Robertson; best cold weather gear for your kids; trust-building in relationships; The Script.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Tommy Smythe’s small space tips; Ramsin’s bathroom design tips; Aly Velji’s favourite Calgary finds.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Hockey Parent Survival Guide.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: LOFT Holiday fashion show; Holiday hair & beauty guide; how to wear metallics.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: A gift guide for homeowners; Marilyn's Jasper road trip adventure; actor Richard Armitage.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Olsen Europe makeover; actors Sean Cullen, Debra DiGiovanni and Darrin Rose.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Real Estate Reality Check with The Property Brothers!

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Mommy Bloggers & how to get deals online; fashion show with online buys; latest style apps.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Sally Field; The Canadian Tenors; Halloween meltdown survival Guide, Halloween nutrition myths.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Lloyd Robertson; best cold weather gear for your kids; trust-building in relationships; The Script.

programme.title: Shockwave
programme.desc: A powerful earthquake and tsunami will some day devastate the Pacific Northwest. What can we do to prepare?

programme.title: Solar Superstorms: Journey to the Center of the Sun
programme.desc: As technology and electrical grids have spread, big solar storms pose a threat equal to the worst hurricanes.

programme.title: NASA'S 10 Greatest Achievements
programme.desc: To get a a leg up in the space race with Russia, the US declared the ultimate goal: put a man on the moon.

programme.title: NASA'S 10 Greatest Achievements
programme.desc: Apollo 8, the reusable space shuttle, the ISS, and the Apollo 13 would all put NASA one step closer.

programme.title: Order and Disorder
programme.desc: Jim investigates a strange set of laws that link together everything from engines to humans to stars.

programme.title: Order and Disorder
programme.desc: Jim learns that information isn't just about human communication; it's woven into the very fabric of reality.

programme.title: Disasters in Space
programme.desc: Relive the harrowing moments in Launch Control during NASA's second "Return to Flight" test flight .

programme.title: Mars Calling: Manifest Destiny or Grand Illusion
programme.desc: This film merges the mythic and the scientific to show that Mars may not be what we imagine.

programme.title: Voyage of Curiosity
programme.desc: Curiosity's robot rover exploring Mars is now known as one of the greatest achievements in space exploration.

programme.title: Search for the Cosmic Dawn: The James Webb Space Telescope
programme.desc: The James Webb Space telescope: on the hunt for the events that gave the universe its start

programme.title: Our World is on Fire
programme.desc: Climate change is now a crisis, and our world is crying out. Examine some possible remedies to the emergency.

programme.title: The Saturn V Story
programme.desc: This is the story of the most powerful machine ever built and a nation who watched on with hope, together.

programme.title: Our Roots, Our Future
programme.desc: Scientists are studying root growth. Roots may help solve climate change and protect the environment.

programme.title: Disasters in Space
programme.desc: As the clock ticks toward the critical Mars launch window, NASA scrambles to fix a critical space mistake.

programme.title: Disasters in Space
programme.desc: Despite multiple pressures, a shocking discovery causes NASA to cancel a historic launch.

programme.title: Disasters in Space
programme.desc: A spacecraft freefalls from orbit smashing into the ground when its parachute fails to deploy.

programme.title: Disasters in Space
programme.desc: A harrowing space mistake leaves the solid rocket boosters hanging by a thread.

programme.title: Disasters in Space
programme.desc: The loss of Space Shuttle Columbia stands as one of the worst disasters in spaceflight history.

programme.title: Asteroids: A New Horizon
programme.desc: Oumuamua, the first interstellar object detected from another star system to come this close to the Earth.

programme.title: Asteroids: A New Horizon
programme.desc: Top space resource companies all vie for competitive advantage. The new space race is just getting started.

programme.title: Space Colonies
programme.desc: Asteroids represent one off-Earth option for human colonization. They may be viable, but would be dangerous.

programme.title: The Next Great Extinction Event
programme.desc: There have been five major extinction events in our planet's history, are we about to experience the 6th?

programme.title: Solar Superstorms: Journey to the Center of the Sun
programme.desc: As technology and electrical grids have spread, big solar storms pose a threat equal to the worst hurricanes.

programme.title: Killer Asteroid
programme.desc: Can a new generation of cutting-edge space missions face the ultimate natural disaster?

programme.title: The Age of Hubble
programme.desc: The Hubble Space Telescope has delivered unprecedented discoveries about the nature of our Universe, and more.

programme.title: Day of the Asteroid
programme.desc: Armed with new defensive technologies, scientists are getting ready for the Day of the Asteroid.

programme.title: The Saturn V Story
programme.desc: This is the story of the most powerful machine ever built and a nation who watched on with hope, together.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: Take an amazing journey from the highest peaks of the Bernese Alps to a spectacular finish at the Rhine Falls.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: The ibex, marmot, and European adder have all developed strategies to survive the harsh alpine winter.

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: Volcanoes can bury entire cities in clouds of ash and gas, or turn rivers into torrents of mud and debris.

programme.title: The Dark Side of the Ocean
programme.desc: One man plans to do what no man has done before. His aim, to unlock the secrets of the dark ocean.

programme.title: Freaks of the Sea
programme.desc: In the oceans of our planet lurk creatures weirder than our wildest dreams and our scariest nightmares.

programme.title: The Global Whale Shark Mystery
programme.desc: Despite its size, the greatest shark in the planet’s oceans, the whale shark, remains an enigma to scientists.

programme.title: Disaster Diaries
programme.desc: 2015 was marked by many exceptional natural disasters. Is this the result of global warming?

programme.title: Disaster Diaries
programme.sub-title: A Year of Floods and Fire (2016)
programme.desc: 2016 marked the end of one of the strongest events ever recorded.

programme.title: Disaster Diaries
programme.desc: In the year 2017 local weather anomalies caused massive flooding. Eyewitnesses describe what happened.

programme.title: Living with Volcanoes
programme.desc: All around the earth, crowds pray and worship volcanoes that are sacred to them.

programme.title: Living with Volcanoes
programme.desc: Japanese fertile lands all attest of the immeasurable wealth hidden beneath the brimstone giants.

programme.title: Living with Volcanoes
programme.desc: Volcanoes have been ripping the earth apart. Are we on the verge of the next Pompeii tragedy?

programme.title: Living with Volcanoes
programme.desc: Volcanologists are ready to face any challenge to quench their thirst for knowledge

programme.title: Voyage of the Continents
programme.sub-title: Oceania: The Tectonic Ring of Fire
programme.desc: Pacific and Australian tectonic plates clash across the South Pacific causing intense volcanic activity

programme.title: Voyage of the Continents
programme.sub-title: Asia: The Tectonics of Life and Death
programme.desc: First Asia hurtled into Europe. They remain bound together by the Urals.

programme.title: Voyage of the Continents
programme.sub-title: Asia: Rising Mountains and Sinking Countries
programme.desc: As the Indo-Asian collision continued into the rest of Asia, the deforming pressure created islands.

programme.title: Voyage of the Continents
programme.desc: One billion years ago there was just one great continent on earth. Then, it began to break apart.

programme.title: Voyage of the Continents
programme.desc: Over time, the Atlantic Ocean widened. Then Africa moved north, struck Europe and birthed the Alps.

programme.title: Our World is on Fire
programme.desc: Climate change is now a crisis, and our world is crying out. Examine some possible remedies to the emergency.

programme.title: Dust Storm
programme.desc: We investigate dust storm causes, and how to predict them and lessen their impact.

programme.title: China Quake
programme.desc: In 2008, China’s monster quake claimed 80,000 lives and destroyed five million buildings.

programme.title: Tsunami: Facing the Global Threat
programme.desc: Tsunamis are so sudden and powerful that in a matter of minutes, they can wipe out a coastline.

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: An asteroid could be barreling toward Earth at any time, now or in the future. Only science can save us.

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: Tornadoes: Can we use modern technology to predict the emergence and path of this violent force of nature?

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Snowy Owl: Queen of the North
programme.desc: No other owls are capable of surviving the extreme conditions, whereas the snowy owl is able to hunt through six months of bright sunshine and six months of twilight. This film follows a pair of snowy owls as they breed on the tundra of the High Arctic, 300 miles inside the Arctic Circle. Lemming numbers are low this year. Five owlets hatch and they face a struggle if any are going to survive.

programme.title: Missing Butterflies
programme.desc: Its miraculous transformation from caterpillar to remarkable winged beauty has been a point of constant intrigue. However, in the last few years, many have observed a decrease in the butterfly population. A much-discussed topic in the entomological society is the decline of the insect populace, but to what extent is this topic relevant to our beloved butterflies? For years, a teacher from lower Bavaria and a biologist from Munich have dedicated themselves to studying the butterfly and their presence in Bavaria. The results of their studies tell an enlightening story that is sure to entertain.

programme.title: Unnatural Selection
programme.desc: The wings of cliff swallows in Nebraska are getting shorter – while female turtles in Chesapeake Bay are getting larger. On the Galapagos Islands, two species of finches are developing back into one. Increasing numbers of peregrine falcons are living in urban areas – and have adapted their behaviour: thanks to the city lights, they have learned to hunt at night.

programme.title: Leap Of The Lynx
programme.desc: On the Iberian Peninsula, primeval landscapes are practically non-existent, so the Iberian lynx has lived on cultivated land for centuries – cattle, horse and pig pastures, cork oak forests, timberland, and even holy sites of pilgrimage. The diversity and beauty of these locations are truly stunning and their character and history as relevant to this story as that of the big cat itself. Until five years ago, both populations of Iberian lynx were quickly fading away. Their fragmented habitats were shrinking even further, they were starved by lack of prey and reduced by epidemics. But now, local authorities, scientists, landowners, and other groups have finally begun to act in unison.

programme.title: Lions On The Move
programme.desc: Kevin Richardson shares an extraordinary bond with Africa’s most fearsome predators. Together with them, he has experienced some real adventures. However, transporting 27 lions and 14 hyenas to a new home presents a different challenge.The lions' new home will be a sanctuary on the edge of Dinokeng, a South African game reserve spanning 18,000 square metres. The huge task ahead includes planning and designing the new enclosures, and securing permits to move the animals.

programme.title: Lions On The Move
programme.desc: Not only are the animals regularly rotated into new enclosures to prevent repetitive behaviour developing out of boredom, but Kevin also has to practice loading them in and out of the van. Can the reluctant lions and hyenas eventually be persuaded to get on board with the move?

programme.title: Elephants: Back to the Wild
programme.sub-title: Elephants: Back to the Wild: The Orphanage of Elephants - Nairobi Nationalpark
programme.desc: We follow the rescue of a baby elephant. Once rescued, it will be transported by plane to the breeding station and raised with love and commitment. In order to satisfy the little pachyderm’s urge to move while in the breeding station, there is sometimes an adorable football match between the animals.

programme.title: Elephants: Back to the Wild
programme.sub-title: Elephants: Back to the Wild: The Return of the Elephants - Tsavo-East-Nationalpark
programme.desc: We accompany the first steps of a two-year-old elephant who is just starting his release program. We also observe the story of two young elephants in their first night in the wilderness on their own. Captivating film footage allows us to truly learn how elephants behave in the wild.

programme.title: Rewilding: Back to Nature
programme.desc: Worldwide, there are various projects and approaches to give nature more space again. The aim is to create larger wilderness areas that develop with hardly any influence of humans - by reintroducing so-called key animal species and creating a network of wilderness corridors across entire continents. Rewilding promises to be a cost-effective form of conservation in which we can boost biodiversity and protect the climate.

programme.title: Wild Prairie Man
programme.desc: Located in the Northern Great Plains of Canada, Grasslands National Park has been a 20 year odyssey for wildlife photographer James R. Page. For James, critters are more interesting than people and he shares some of his iconic images and experiences from this endangered ecosystem. We showcase the parks wilderness that is dominated by prairie light and dark sky, the wildlife, prairie dogs, mesmerizing landscapes, and bisons. We immerse ourselves in the wild habitat of Grasslands National Park for over a year capturing some never before scenes in the Wild Prairie. James R. Page's connection to the land and the wildlife provides an unforgettable journey into the heart of this endangered ecosystem, while Park experts help us understand its complexity.

programme.title: The Secret of Migrating Birds - Tryst at the Red Sea
programme.desc: Each year in spring, Israel witnesses a unique spectacle. Many millions of migrating birds cross the land bridge between Africa, Asia and Europe.Their first major roosting place after an exhausting nonstop flight over the African deserts is in southern Israel – a vital stopover. Here they replenish their energy reserves. The flocks of migrating birds are pursued by flocks of ornithologists. Hundreds of birders flood into the port city of Eilat on the Red Sea to take part in the Migration Festival. For a week, Israel's southernmost coastal resort is home to international scientists and amateur birders.

programme.title: King of the Mountains - Golden Eagle
programme.desc: Aerial shots of mating Golden eagles, a clash between a Golden and a White-tailed eagle, and an intimate glimpse into their nesting grounds combine to create a spectacular film. Tracking these majestic birds reveals the wild nature of their hunting grounds, showcasing the diverse animal and plant life that share their habitat with the 'king of the skies.' Viewers are brought up close as the eagle effortlessly glides over forests and canyons, abruptly diving in a breathtaking pursuit of prey. Witnessing these birds in their natural habitats is witnessing them at their finest. Embark on a magnificent journey through the skies and landscapes with us.

programme.title: King of the Seas - Sea Eagle
programme.desc: The times where eagles have been seen as varmints are over. They are now perceived as an integral part of the food cycle. Due to the fact that the habitats of the sea eagles in Germany are largely found in the north-eastern regions that are rich in water, the movie accompanies the eagles to an island, home to hundreds of sea ravens, where they rob the baby sea ravens of their food. They seem to always profit from the successes of the fellow creatures in their habitats. This film introduces you to the expert poachers of the animal kingdom.

programme.title: Hummingbirds: Jewelled Messengers
programme.desc: Hummingbirds live at the limits of what is possible. They have the highest metabolism with a heart rate of 1,200 beats per minute of any vertebrate. Their wings beat 50 times a second. They can hover on the spot, and even fly backwards. To our eyes, they are just a blur. But with the aid of high-speed cameras we can slow down their hyperactive lives and look into their secret world.

programme.title: Snowy Owl: Queen of the North
programme.desc: No other owls are capable of surviving the extreme conditions, whereas the snowy owl is able to hunt through six months of bright sunshine and six months of twilight. This film follows a pair of snowy owls as they breed on the tundra of the High Arctic, 300 miles inside the Arctic Circle. Lemming numbers are low this year. Five owlets hatch and they face a struggle if any are going to survive.

programme.title: Missing Butterflies
programme.desc: Its miraculous transformation from caterpillar to remarkable winged beauty has been a point of constant intrigue. However, in the last few years, many have observed a decrease in the butterfly population. A much-discussed topic in the entomological society is the decline of the insect populace, but to what extent is this topic relevant to our beloved butterflies? For years, a teacher from lower Bavaria and a biologist from Munich have dedicated themselves to studying the butterfly and their presence in Bavaria. The results of their studies tell an enlightening story that is sure to entertain.

programme.title: Unnatural Selection
programme.desc: The wings of cliff swallows in Nebraska are getting shorter – while female turtles in Chesapeake Bay are getting larger. On the Galapagos Islands, two species of finches are developing back into one. Increasing numbers of peregrine falcons are living in urban areas – and have adapted their behaviour: thanks to the city lights, they have learned to hunt at night.

programme.title: Leap Of The Lynx
programme.desc: On the Iberian Peninsula, primeval landscapes are practically non-existent, so the Iberian lynx has lived on cultivated land for centuries – cattle, horse and pig pastures, cork oak forests, timberland, and even holy sites of pilgrimage. The diversity and beauty of these locations are truly stunning and their character and history as relevant to this story as that of the big cat itself. Until five years ago, both populations of Iberian lynx were quickly fading away. Their fragmented habitats were shrinking even further, they were starved by lack of prey and reduced by epidemics. But now, local authorities, scientists, landowners, and other groups have finally begun to act in unison.

programme.title: Lions On The Move
programme.desc: Kevin Richardson shares an extraordinary bond with Africa’s most fearsome predators. Together with them, he has experienced some real adventures. However, transporting 27 lions and 14 hyenas to a new home presents a different challenge.The lions' new home will be a sanctuary on the edge of Dinokeng, a South African game reserve spanning 18,000 square metres. The huge task ahead includes planning and designing the new enclosures, and securing permits to move the animals.

programme.title: Lions On The Move
programme.desc: Not only are the animals regularly rotated into new enclosures to prevent repetitive behaviour developing out of boredom, but Kevin also has to practice loading them in and out of the van. Can the reluctant lions and hyenas eventually be persuaded to get on board with the move?

programme.title: Elephants: Back to the Wild
programme.sub-title: Elephants: Back to the Wild: The Orphanage of Elephants - Nairobi Nationalpark
programme.desc: We follow the rescue of a baby elephant. Once rescued, it will be transported by plane to the breeding station and raised with love and commitment. In order to satisfy the little pachyderm’s urge to move while in the breeding station, there is sometimes an adorable football match between the animals.

programme.title: Elephants: Back to the Wild
programme.sub-title: Elephants: Back to the Wild: The Return of the Elephants - Tsavo-East-Nationalpark
programme.desc: We accompany the first steps of a two-year-old elephant who is just starting his release program. We also observe the story of two young elephants in their first night in the wilderness on their own. Captivating film footage allows us to truly learn how elephants behave in the wild.

programme.title: Asia - Secret Lives Hidden Places II
programme.sub-title: Asia - Secret Lives Hidden Places II: Gaoligongshan Park: China – Himalaya’s Gold
programme.desc: The Great Gobi National Park in southwestern Mongolia shelters one of the last populations of wild Bactrian camels, amid its ancient and mysterious desert landscape. With around 800 critically endangered animals, the Gobi remains one of the world's most sparsely inhabited and inhospitable regions, yet its allure is undeniable, offering a captivating backdrop for encounters with wild camels and stories of people deeply connected to nature.

programme.title: Asia - Secret Lives Hidden Places II
programme.sub-title: Asia - Secret Lives Hidden Places II: Great Gobi National Park: Mongolia – Following the Wild Camels
programme.desc: The jungle in northern Vietnam is nearly impenetrable, with some areas never touched by humans. The remote rainforests of Pu Mat National Park serve as a refuge for endangered wildlife such as black bears, monkeys, and pangolins. Tuan, a biologist and bear specialist, embarks on an expedition to these untouched regions of the park in search of bears. The most hunted animal here is the pangolin, sought after for its scales believed to have medicinal properties and its meat considered a delicacy. A small Vietnamese conservation organization fights for the survival of this species.

programme.title: Dreams of the Settlers - An American Journey
programme.sub-title: Through Utah's Canyons to Salt Lake City
programme.desc: It’s a dream route leading from the casino metropolis of Las Vegas, to Salt Lake City, the Mormon capital. It is a journey that follows the footsteps of the first settlers: the Mormons. They came to this desert belt of the United States during the second half of the 19th century to escape persecution and in search of the promised land. Some of them founded Las Vegas. Others moved through the canyons and over the mountains of the colossal Colorado Plateau, where they found water and pasture, to establish many small settlements. The landscapes through which they trekked, had and still have, a breathtaking and bizarre majestic beauty. Bryce Canyon, the hogbacks and the Escalante Staircase plateau are dramatic scenes of wonder formed from rock. The Pajute Indians once described Bryce Canyon as: “red rocks standing like men in a basin”.

programme.title: Penguin Baywatch
programme.desc: Macaroni penguins in Antarctica form strong family bonds, facing predators and playful fur seals.

programme.title: Super Senses
programme.desc: In this episode we'll explore the animal sense of sight

programme.title: Beak & Brain - Genius Birds From Down Under
programme.desc: These child prodigies of the animal kingdom have feathers and beaks - and very special skills!

programme.title: Parrot's Paradise
programme.desc: Aaron Pedersen explores the unique Australian characteristics that have facilitated the thriving of parrots

programme.title: Super Senses
programme.desc: Living in the aerial realm requires a unique set of senses

programme.title: Eagle - Queen Of The Skies
programme.desc: To explore the world of the eagle we follow the fortunes of one individual from hatching to leaving the nest.

programme.title: Storks - A Village Rooftop Saga
programme.desc: Ruhstadt, Germany, hosts Germany's largest white stork colony, despite facing damage to roads, roofs

programme.title: Circus In The Sky
programme.desc: With their acrobatic aerial plays, birds of prey make some of the most impressive appearances in the sky.

programme.title: Yellowstone Journals
programme.desc: Yellowstone's creatures thrive due to unique abilities, anatomies, and senses, especially in Spring.

programme.title: The Birds of Winter
programme.desc: Birds, including Arctic foxes, cranes, and swans, prepare for their winter journey.

programme.title: A Southern Winter
programme.desc: The south of the Iberian Peninsula thrives with migrants, wildlife, and a plentiful winter.

programme.title: Primeval New Zealand
programme.desc: Peter Elliott reveals new information about the origins New Zealands 'evolutionary mutants'.

programme.title: Iceland, The Newborn Island
programme.desc: Curious Arctic fox faces dangers in a new world, its winter memories revived by the sun.

programme.title: Spring. The Return of Life
programme.desc: The Iberian Peninsula's stunning spring in 40 years, featuring rare wildlife moments.

programme.title: Wild Italy: Living With Volcanoes
programme.desc: Italy's ancient volcanoes now have sulfurous gas, lakes, and the Manzanar caldera with birch trees.

programme.title: Wild Italy: Living With Volcanoes
programme.desc: Italy's ancient volcanoes formed lakes 200,000 years ago; Mount Etna's eruption affected all life.

programme.title: Wild Italy: Living With Volcanoes
programme.desc: Italy's volcanic origins created islands like Stromboli, Lipari, and Vulcano, both continental and oceanic.

programme.title: The Unknown Snake Of Bolivia
programme.desc: Herpetologists Juan Tims and Hiram Cuevas explore Bolivia to find venomous snakes and a new viper.

programme.title: The Unknown Snake Of Bolivia
programme.desc: The expedition starts in Samay Pata, then moves to Bella Vista's palm forest for supplies.

programme.title: Saliega's Lineage, The Return Of The Iberian Lynx
programme.desc: The Iberian lynx was saved by captive females San Diego and Ora, and their newborns.

programme.title: Jerte, Wildlife In The Cherry Valley
programme.desc: Southwest Spain's snow-covered valley, with goats, cherries, and Hatay river, is a must-see.

programme.title: Island Of The Hippos
programme.desc: A 3-month-old hippo prince is born in West Africa's B Songo Soca, protected by mothers.

programme.title: I'm The Best Iberian Red Deer
programme.desc: Iberian stags battle eagles and vultures in harsh weather, preparing nests for breeding.

programme.title: Dehesa
programme.desc: The Iberian Peninsula, rich in ecosystem and dance, attracts visitors like acorn-fed pigs.

programme.title: Giant Sea Serpent, Meet The Myth
programme.desc: Scientific boys off French Riviera uncover secrets of the giant oarfish, likely the sea serpent myth's source.

programme.title: Iceland, The Newborn Island
programme.desc: Curious Arctic fox faces dangers in a new world, its winter memories revived by the sun.

programme.title: Spring. The Return of Life
programme.desc: The Iberian Peninsula's stunning spring in 40 years, featuring rare wildlife moments.

programme.title: The National
programme.desc: The National is CBC's flagship nightly news program, featuring the day's top stories with in-depth journalism.

programme.title: CBC News: Atlantic Tonight
programme.desc: Host Amy Smith brings you breaking news, in-depth reports and weather you can count on.

programme.title: CBC News: Atlantic Tonight
programme.desc: Host Amy Smith brings you breaking news, in-depth reports and weather you can count on.

programme.title: CBC News: Atlantic Tonight

programme.title: What If
programme.desc: Take an epic journey through hypothetical worlds and possibilities.

programme.title: How To Survive
programme.desc: Endure life-threatening moments by employing statistical facts and survivor stories, all while entertaining.

programme.title: Survivorman: Bigfoot
programme.desc: Something that is supposed to weigh 900 pounds has to leave a trail – whatever is there, it leaves tracks.

programme.title: Dream In Colour
programme.desc: Get lost in the breathtaking beauty of Canadian and international landscapes

programme.title: Dream In Colour
programme.desc: Get lost in the breathtaking beauty of Canadian and international landscapes

programme.title: Dream In Colour
programme.desc: Get lost in the breathtaking beauty of Canadian and international landscapes

programme.title: Dream In Colour

programme.title: Dream In Colour
programme.desc: Get lost in the breathtaking beauty of Canadian and international landscapes

programme.title: Dream In Colour
programme.desc: Get lost in the breathtaking beauty of Canadian and international landscapes

programme.title: Bucketlist Africa
programme.desc: Africa: one of the most iconic and diverse continents. It's packed with a spectacular range of habitats and environments, and home to some of the best loved and dramatic wildlife on the planet. From the small and surprising to the enormous and entertaining, there's something for everyone's bucket list on this vast continent. From camels to giraffes, gorillas to buffalo, the sheer range of wildlife found in Africa is unbelievable. With so much to see, where would you begin your bucket list adventure?

programme.title: Enchanted Forest
programme.desc: There are few places on the planet more magical than the forest. To spend a day here is nature's way of offering a spa, a bathe in nature. There is enchantment at every turn.

programme.title: Aging in the Wild
programme.desc: In this episode, we explore how different animal species react to impending death. We explore the factors that lead animals to their final resting places: disease, injury, predation, drought, famine.

programme.title: Europe's Wilderness
programme.desc: The Bearded Vulture is one of the largest birds of prey in Europe and one of the rarest. With its habitat above the treeline in the alpine mountains, we see one of the largest airworthy birds fly loops during its courtship days and flight for survival on the steep slopes.

programme.title: Europe's Wilderness
programme.desc: The Grey Seal is Europe's largest water prediator. With its habitat in the fish rich costal areas in cool but not arctic waters we discover that recreation in the sun plays a major role for the seal, but we also see it raiding in dangerous waters.

programme.title: Uncharted Amazon
programme.desc: Deep in the jungles of Peru, a silky anteater is fighting to stay awake and a mother humming bird is struggling to raise her chick. The seasonal downpours are about to begin and the rainforest's animals must strive to cope with the changing conditions. Through millions of years of evolution they have developed bizarre relationships and unexpected strategies to overcome them, but for all their ingenuity, they were never prepared for the arrival of a new species on the scene.

programme.title: Frontier Vets
programme.desc: The group discovers Roxanne's childhood fear of lizards. Niven takes the opportunity to exploit it.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: Astrobiology is a visionary new science that searches for life in space.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: The magical liquids of our Liquid Universe.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: Explore the science behind the seemingly fanciful notion of warp-drive.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: Space colonization is no longer the fodder of science fiction.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: Scientists are trying to confirm that other holes lurk in hyperspace.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: We are in the midst of the greatest era of space discovery.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: Probe the physiological, psychological and cultural challenges of sex in space.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: Astrobiology is a visionary new science that searches for life in space.
